issues: dark L-shaped cobble in upper quadrant (~col 5-12, row 6-14) repeats as a faint polka-dot anchor when tiled 3x3
small salmon/pink cluster near upper-left slightly eye-catching across tile repetitions
reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Column 0 vs col 31 — background tan is consistent across both edges; no hard seam break visible in the side-by-side tiled render. (2) Row 0 vs row 31 — top and bottom edges continue smoothly; no obvious color band discontinuity. (3) 3x3 mental tiling: the dark charcoal L/bracket-shaped cobble element in the upper portion is the most distinct feature and would repeat at regular intervals, though it is small enough that it does not dominate. The accent color dots (pink, orange, teal, yellow) are scattered well and do not cluster into a single hot-spot. Visual density is balanced — no large empty swaths. Background khaki/tan is muted and would not fight foreground sprites. Overall this reads convincingly as a cobblestone path tile, but the recurring L-feature prevents a clean pass.
